title: mentha spicata var. oblongifolia
intro: Mentha spicata var. oblongifolia, also known as American spearmint, is a subspecies of the spearmint plant native to North America. It is characterized by its broad, oval-shaped leaves and purple flowers, which are often used to create herbal remedies. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds, and has been used to alleviate digestive issues, relieve headaches, and soothe respiratory problems. American spearmint can be used in various herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and essential oils, which can be used to promote relaxation, improve digestion, and support overall health.
